title: The two faces of democracy: decentering agonism and deliberation [Book Review]
author: Rozenburg

opening_move.description: The review opens by affirming the need to bridge deliberative and agonist theories of democracy, positioning Scudder and White's book as an attempt to hold both legitimate democratic modes simultaneously.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Democratic theory needs to account for both legitimacy-through-reason and conflict-through-struggle; neither model alone is sufficient

theoretical_positioning.description: The review argues that Scudder and White's 'communicative model' ultimately fails to integrate the two models, and offers an independent diagnosis of why the synthesis is unsuccessful.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: conceptual analysis of deliberative and agonist models
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review; the reviewer's own position (that the synthesis fails) is the substantive contribution. The argument is conducted entirely within conceptual-normative space.

case_type.case_name: Scudder and White, The Two Faces of Democracy (2023)

confidence_notes: Book review genre with strong reviewer intervention — reviewer argues the synthesis fails. The immanent critique of Scudder and White is the independent contribution.
